title: Hydrogen Fuel Cell Industry Challenges and Market Outlook 2024-2025
text: The hydrogen and fuel cell industry faced significant challenges in 2024-2025 with prolonged policy uncertainty leading to multi-year push-outs in hydrogen project development and fuel cell application deployment. Broader hydrogen fuel cell sector faces persistent obstacles including hydrogen-powered vehicles grappling with infrastructure bottlenecks, high costs, and highly niche market segments, especially in light vehicles. A shortage of hydrogen refueling stations remains a major barrier to widespread FCEV adoption, with about 1,160 HRS in operation worldwide by end of 2024, compared to around 4.5 million EV charging stations. Current hydrogen prices at retail stations average $16-18 per kilogram. Compound challenges include high production costs, limited infrastructure, and consumer awareness gaps. Industry consolidation occurred with company failures, particularly among smaller integrators in the truck market, causing adoption timelines to materially push out. Policy uncertainty in the US, combined with difficult funding environments, led to broader industry rationalization. Despite challenges, the bus segment emerged as the most mature application with sustained demand. Companies like BLDP focused strategic efforts on bus-market players with platform wins from major OEMs, representing the strongest revenue growth area amid broader headwinds.
